steps.title: Identify the central contrast in the claim
steps.content: The student contrasts one age group where popularity peaked against older groups where it declined. To support this, the data must show a younger age group consistently having higher participation than an older age group throughout 1992–1995.

steps.title: Locate this contrast in the table
steps.content:
Brownies (6–8 years) are the peak participation group, consistently outnumbering Seniors (14–17 years) by orders of magnitude: 1,319 versus 50, 1,225 versus 43, 1,181 versus 45, 1,142 versus 52.

Choice D precisely documents this: for each year, fewer Seniors than Brownies.

The other choices either contradict the data (A says Ambassadors exceeded Brownies once, which never happened; C says Daisies always exceeded Ambassadors, which is false) or isolate Brownies' decline without the age-group comparison (B).

This age-based contrast directly supports the claim.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
